name: Stargate Abilene (Flagship Campus)
notes: Flagship Stargate site. First 2 buildings (400 MW) operational Oct 2025; full 8-building 1.2 GW campus by mid-2026. Built by Crusoe Energy on Lancium Clean Campus. 4M sq ft total.

name: Stargate Shackelford County
notes: One of 5 new Stargate sites announced Sep 2025. Oracle-led. Part of 5.5 GW expansion tranche with Dona Ana and Midwest sites.

name: Stargate Milam County
notes: SoftBank-led Stargate site in central Texas. Can scale to 1.5 GW over 18 months from groundbreaking.

name: Stargate Dona Ana County
notes: Oracle-led Stargate site in southern New Mexico near Las Cruces. Part of 5.5 GW expansion.

name: Stargate Lordstown
notes: SoftBank-led site at former Lordstown Motors plant in Ohio. Groundbreaking complete, expected operational 2027.

name: Stargate Lighthouse (Port Washington, WI)
notes: Codenamed Lighthouse. 4 data centers on 674 acres, 902 MW IT capacity. $15B investment. WEC Energy providing ~2 GW total power. Completion 2028.

name: xAI Colossus (Memphis)
notes: World's largest AI training facility. 150K H100 + 50K H200 + 30K GB200 GPUs. Three buildings across Memphis/Southaven. Expanded to 2 GW in Jan 2026. Roadmap to 1M GPUs.

name: xAI MACROHARDRR (Southaven, MS)
notes: Third xAI data center in greater Memphis area. $20B investment, largest economic development in MS history. Operations expected Feb 2026. Near xAI-owned power plant.

name: Meta Hyperion (Richland Parish, LA)
notes: Massive 4M+ sq ft AI supercluster on 2,250 acres. Phase 1 is 2 GW, expandable to 5 GW. $10B initial investment, $27B JV with Blue Owl. Construction started late 2024. Entergy building new gas plants.

name: Meta Prometheus (New Albany, OH)
notes: World's first 1 GW data center when complete. Purpose-built for AI training/serving. Nuclear power deals with TerraPower, Oklo, Vistra. Due online 2026.

name: Meta El Paso Data Center
notes: AI-optimized data center scalable to 1 GW. Initial $1.5B investment growing to $10B+. Meta's 29th data center.

name: Meta Beaver Dam Data Center
notes: Meta's 26th US data center, 30th worldwide. Located in Beaver Dam, Wisconsin.

name: Microsoft Fairwater (Mount Pleasant, WI)
notes: Described as world's most powerful AI datacenter. Phase 1 at 400 MW, scaling to 900 MW. $3.3B initial investment. 1.2M sq ft across 3 buildings on 315 acres.

name: Microsoft Mount Pleasant Phase 2
notes: Second datacenter of similar size/scale to Fairwater. Additional $4B investment bringing WI total to $7B+. Combined sites could reach 9 GW at full build.

name: Microsoft Cheyenne Data Center
notes: North Range Business Park campus. 200 MW capacity. Completion targeted early 2026. Powered partly by Black Hills Energy wind projects.

name: Microsoft Phoenix/Goodyear AZ Campus
notes: Multiple facilities in El Mirage and Goodyear, AZ. Part of Microsoft's broader Azure cloud and AI infrastructure in the West Valley.

name: Microsoft Plano/Chicago IL Campus
notes: 500 acres acquired in Plano, IL area (Kendall County). Part of North Central US region expansion with additional availability zones by end of 2026.

name: Amazon Project Rainier (New Carlisle, IN)
notes: World's largest cluster of non-Nvidia AI chips. 1,200 acres, 30 buildings planned at 2.2 GW. 7 buildings operational Oct 2025. Built for Anthropic. $11B initial, $15B total IN investment.

name: AWS Mattermeade (Caroline/Spotsylvania, VA)
notes: 1,143 acres across 13 parcels near I-95. 11 two-story data center buildings planned. 770 MW operational capacity target by 2027.

name: AWS Vicksburg (Mississippi)
notes: $3B investment, largest private-sector investment in Warren County history. Part of AWS expansion into new states.

name: AWS Arlington Oregon Campus
notes: 400+ acres secured in Arlington, OR for future data center campus. Adds to existing Oregon footprint in Morrow and Umatilla counties.

name: AWS Northern Virginia (Ashburn cluster)
notes: AWS US East (N. Virginia) is AWS's largest region. Dozens of facilities in Loudoun/Prince William counties. Expanding into Maryland with new AZ in 2026.

name: Google Council Bluffs Iowa Campus
notes: Operational since 2009, continuously expanded. $6.8B invested in Iowa to date. 407 MW wind power. $7B expansion pledge with new Cedar Rapids campus.

name: Google The Dalles Oregon Campus
notes: Google's first owned data center, operational since 2006. $1.8B+ invested. $600M expansion underway with new 290K sq ft building.

name: Google Mayes County Oklahoma Campus
notes: Mayes 2 is 200 MW. $5.7B invested in OK. $9B two-year investment announced with 2 new Muskogee County campuses and 600 MW of new clean energy PPAs.

name: Google Muskogee County Oklahoma (New)
notes: Two new data center campuses in Muskogee County as part of $9B Oklahoma investment. 600 MW of new clean energy PPAs.

name: Google Midlothian Texas Campus
notes: Part of $40B Texas commitment through 2027 for 3 new data centers. Original DFW-area campus built 2019. Expanding AI/cloud capacity.

name: Google Pine Island Minnesota
notes: Google's first MN data center. Will deploy 1,400 MW wind, 200 MW solar, 300 MW battery storage via Xcel. Renewables online 2028-2029.

name: Google Kansas City (Project Mica)
notes: In-development Google facility. 500-acre site off NE corner of I-435. Part of broader PJM Interconnection AI infrastructure investment.

name: CoreWeave Weehawken NJ (LGA1)
notes: CoreWeave's US East facility near NYC. Part of 32 data centers with 250K total GPUs across the fleet.

name: CoreWeave Kenilworth NJ
notes: $1.8B purpose-built data center. First award under NJ EDA tax credit program. Construction started mid-Sep 2025, operational early 2027.

name: CoreWeave Lancaster PA
notes: $6B purpose-built facility. 15-year, $11B capacity commitment. One of CoreWeave's largest planned campuses.

name: CoreWeave Las Vegas (LAS1)
notes: CoreWeave's West Coast GPU cloud facility serving West Coast AI/ML workloads.

name: CoreWeave Chester VA
notes: CoreWeave Virginia facility. Part of broader US footprint supporting AI/ML inference and training workloads.

name: CoreWeave/Core Scientific Muskogee OK
notes: CoreWeave and Core Scientific data center at Port of Muskogee. Part of CoreWeave's acquisition of Core Scientific (1.3 GW portfolio).

name: Applied Digital Ellendale ND (Polaris Forge 1)
notes: First 100 MW building fully energized Q4 2025. Part of 400 MW Ellendale campus. Leased to CoreWeave for AI/HPC.

name: Applied Digital Ellendale ND (Building 2)
notes: Second 150 MW building under construction. Expected ready for service mid-2026. Third 150 MW building in planning (2027).

name: Applied Digital Jamestown ND (JMS01)
notes: 100 MW AI/HPC facility in Jamestown, ND. Applied Digital has PSC approval for up to 530 MW across ND operations.

name: Crusoe Abilene TX (Lancium Clean Campus)
notes: Same physical campus as Stargate Abilene flagship. Crusoe is the builder/operator. 8 buildings, 4M sq ft, 1.2 GW. First 2 buildings energized H1 2025.

name: Crusoe Project Jade (Cheyenne, WY)
notes: 1.8 GW initial campus near Terry Ranch Rd, Laramie County. Scalable to 10 GW. Tallgrass investing $7B in adjacent gas power + CO2 sequestration. Total project cost up to $50B. First buildings by 2027.

name: Crusoe Sparks NV (Redwood Materials)
notes: 24 Crusoe Spark modular data centers powered by Redwood Energy 20 MW microgrid. Proof-of-concept for modular AI factory model.

name: Lambda Vernon CA (Prime LAX01)
notes: GPU cluster at Prime's LAX01 datacenter. Lambda aims for 1M+ NVIDIA GPUs and 3 GW of liquid-cooled capacity. Raised $1.5B in late 2025.

name: Lambda Allen TX (Cologix)
notes: Lambda GPU cloud facility in Allen, TX (DFW area). Part of Lambda's 15+ US data center network.

name: Lambda Columbus OH (Cologix)
notes: Lambda GPU cloud facility in Columbus, OH. Part of distributed US footprint.

name: Oracle Abilene TX (Stargate Cloud Infra)
notes: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure at Stargate Abilene. First 64K GB200 GPUs deployed, scaling to 450K. Oracle purchased ~400K GB200s ($40B) for 15-year OpenAI lease.

name: Oracle Michigan Data Center
notes: New OCI data center in Michigan. Part of Oracle's surge in capex to $35B in 2026 for AI/cloud infrastructure.

name: Microsoft Salem Township PA
notes: Part of $20B Pennsylvania AI/cloud investment. Adjacent to Susquehanna nuclear power plant with direct power connection.

name: Microsoft Falls Township PA
notes: Second key PA site for Microsoft's $20B investment. Part of PJM Interconnection region buildout.

name: Apple Houston AI Server Factory
notes: 250K sq ft AI server manufacturing facility. Produces custom servers for Private Cloud Compute (Apple Intelligence). First test unit Jul 2025, shipping Oct 2025. Part of $500B+ US commitment.

name: STACK Infrastructure Ashburn VA (144 MW)
notes: Two 72 MW data center buildings in Greater Ashburn, Loudoun County. Hyperscale facility in Data Center Alley.

name: STACK Infrastructure Leesburg VA (NVA-06)
notes: 52-acre campus with two buildings (48 MW + 24 MW) in two phases. Loudoun County, VA.

name: Vantage Northern Virginia Expansion
notes: $2B expansion with 3 data centers totaling 192 MW and 929K sq ft in Northern Virginia.

name: Vantage Texas Campus (1.4 GW)
notes: 1,200-acre site hosting 10 data centers at 250 kW+ per rack. $25B+ investment. One of the largest planned single-campus facilities in the US.

name: QTS Cedar Rapids Iowa Campus
notes: $10B data center campus announced Sep 2025. Major hyperscale facility in Iowa.

name: Switch Las Vegas Core Campus
notes: Currently 275 MW, scaling to 495 MW at full build-out. AI factories under construction using EVO design with hybrid air/liquid cooling at 2 MW+ per cabinet.

name: Switch Tahoe Reno Citadel Campus
notes: 7.2M+ sq ft campus with 650 MW capacity. AI factory construction underway across all Switch campuses.

name: Digital Realty Ashburn VA Campus
notes: Part of Digital Realty's 2.8 GW portfolio. Joint venture with Blackstone for $7B in hyperscale campuses. NVIDIA AI Factory Research Center partner.

name: Core Scientific Denton TX (CoreWeave)
notes: Former bitcoin mining campus converted to AI datacenter. 70 MW expansion to 260 MW total. $1.2B contracted revenue. 12-year CoreWeave lease. Part of 590 MW total CoreWeave commitment across 6 sites.

name: Core Scientific Calvert City KY (CoreWeave)
notes: Part of Core Scientific's 6-site, 590 MW CoreWeave hosting portfolio. Converted from crypto mining to AI compute.

name: Core Scientific Dallas TX (New Campus)
notes: Land acquired outside Dallas for 285 MW campus. Part of CoreWeave acquisition of Core Scientific (1.3 GW portfolio + 1 GW expansion potential).

name: Equinix Ashburn VA Campus
notes: Part of Equinix's 270 global datacenters. 100+ MW of Bloom Energy fuel cells deployed across US sites. 59 construction projects underway in 2025.

name: Google Chesterfield VA Data Center
notes: New Google datacenter joining Loudoun/Prince William County facilities as part of Google's Virginia cluster.

name: Meta Gallatin TN Data Center
notes: Operational Meta datacenter supporting AI training workloads. Part of Meta's fleet of 25+ US datacenters.

name: Meta DeKalb IL Data Center
notes: Operational Meta datacenter in northern Illinois. Part of broader Meta AI training infrastructure.

name: Microsoft Fairwater Atlanta
notes: World's first 'AI superfactory' — two-story design, no UPS or gen-sets. Southern Company grid.

name: Microsoft Abilene TX Expansion
notes: Two new AI factory buildings plus 900 MW on-site gas power plant. Adjacent to Stargate campus.

name: Meta Temple TX
notes: 900K sq ft hyperscale campus on 384 acres. Originally paused in 2022, construction resumed.

name: AWS Richmond County, NC
notes: $10B investment, 1,200 acres, 20 buildings. Near Duke Energy's 2.24 GW Smith Jr. power plant.

name: Google Armstrong County TX
notes: Part of Google's $40B Texas commitment. Air-cooled facility in the Panhandle.

name: Google Haskell County TX
notes: Two data center campuses. One co-located with Intersect 840 MW solar + 1.3 GWh storage.

name: EdgeCore Mesa AZ Campus
notes: 84 acres. 206 MW commissioned since Apr 2023, expanding to 450 MW. Powered by Salt River Project.

name: Compass Meridian MS Campus
notes: $10B investment, 8 data center buildings at 40 MW each. One of largest in the Southeast.

name: Compass Red Oak TX Campus
notes: 300 acres, 10 buildings at full build. DFW metro area.

name: CyrusOne Whitney TX (DFW10)
notes: Adjacent to Calpine's 800 MW Bosque Power Plant. 190 MW Calpine power agreement.

name: CyrusOne Fort Worth TX (DFW7)
notes: Partnership with Eolian for 100 MW BESS co-located.

name: QTS Phoenix AZ (PHX2)
notes: Multi-building hyperscale campus. Part of Blackstone's massive QTS expansion.

name: QTS Manassas VA Campus
notes: Six-building, 105-acre campus in Northern Virginia.

name: Prime Data Centers Avondale AZ
notes: 66 acres, 5 data centers, 1.3M sq ft. Lambda partnership for AI GPU infrastructure.

name: Google Red Oak TX (Ellis County)
notes: Part of Google's $40B Texas commitment. First building operational Nov 2025.

name: Aligned Data Centers DFW
notes: Being acquired for $40B by BlackRock, NVIDIA, Microsoft, xAI consortium. Key DFW campus.

name: Riot Platforms Rockdale (Whinstone)
notes: Largest Bitcoin mining facility in North America. 750 MW capacity. Participates in ERCOT demand response — curtails during peak grid stress. Potential AI repurposing candidate.

name: Riot Platforms Corsicana TX
notes: 1 GW planned at full build. 600 MW Phase 1 operational. Immersion-cooled mining. Near Navarro County substation.

name: Marathon Digital Granbury TX
notes: Large-scale Bitcoin mining. Marathon is exploring AI/HPC hosting to diversify revenue.

name: CleanSpark Sandersville GA
notes: Largest CleanSpark facility. 400 MW across multiple buildings. Georgia Power grid.

name: Bitdeer Rockdale TX
notes: Adjacent to Riot Rockdale campus. Mining + cloud hash rate services. Building custom ASIC chips.

name: MARA (Marathon) Garden City TX
notes: West Texas mining site. Cheap wind/solar power. Marathon pursuing AI/HPC diversification.

name: Cipher Mining Odessa TX
notes: West Texas mining facility. Near Permian Basin wind resources. Cipher exploring AI hosting.

name: Cipher Mining Black Pearl TX
notes: Tom Green County. Bitcoin mining with demand response participation.

name: Iris Energy Childress TX
notes: Hybrid crypto/AI facility. 600 MW planned. Iris Energy pivoting to AI/HPC — already hosting NVIDIA GPUs alongside miners.

name: Hut 8 Salt Creek TX
notes: Former US Bitcoin Corp site. Hut 8 actively converting capacity to AI/HPC hosting.
